title: Add & subtract matrices
description: Practice your understanding of the process of adding or subtracting two matrices.

hints: To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the sum:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
0 & -2 \\\\
0 & -2 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the sum:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
7 & 1 \\\\
3 & -3 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the sum:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
1 & -1 \\\\
5 & -2 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the sum:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2 & 6 \\\\
2 & 4 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the difference:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-2 & -1 \\\\
1 & 1 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the difference:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
3 & -2 \\\\
-1 & 3 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the difference:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2 & -1 \\\\
-5 & 1 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the difference:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-2 & -1 \\\\
6 & 0 
\end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following matrix addition.**

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
x & -1 \\\\
-4 & 5 \\\\
3 & -2 
\end{array} \right]
+
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
1 & -1 \\\\
3 & y \\\\
-2 & -3 
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
1 & -2 \\\\
-1 & 9 \\\\
1 & -5 
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=0
\\\\
y&=4
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following matrix addition.**

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2 & 3 \\\\
-1 & x
\end{array} \right]
+
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
y & -5 \\\\
4 & -2 
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
0 & -2 \\\\
3 & 2
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=4
\\\\
y&=-2
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following matrix subtraction.**

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
3 & x & -2
\\\\
-1 & 0 & 4
\end{array} \right]
-
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-2 & 3 & 0
\\\\
5 & 1 & y
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
5 & 2 & -2
\\\\
-6 & -1 & 5
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=5
\\\\
y&=-1
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following matrix subtraction.**

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
5
\\\\
x
\\\\
-1
\end{array} \right]
-
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-3
 \\\\
2
\\\\
y
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
8
\\\\
-5
\\\\
-6
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=-3
\\\\
y&=5
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Here are four matrices:

$\begin{align}
\bold A=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
5 & -6 & 0
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold B=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-3 & 2
\\\\
1 & -2
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
\bold C=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-4 & 1 & -1
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold D=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
5 & 0 & -2
\\\\
-4 & 2 & 1 
\\\\
4 & -2 & 3
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

**Which of the following calculations are possible?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can add or subtract two matrices if they have the same dimensions. Otherwise, the operation is not possible.

Matrics $\bold A$ and $\bold C$ are both $1\times 3$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Other combinations are not possible.

This is the possible calculation:

- $\bold A-\bold C$

assessment_items.question_content:
Here are four matrices:

$\begin{align}
\bold A=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
1 & 2 & 1
\\\\
3 & 0 & 2 
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold B=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2 & 1
\\\\
3 & 6
\\\\
1 & 0
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
\bold C=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2 & -1
\\\\
-1 & 0
\\\\
4 & 1
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold D=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
1 & -2 & 3
\\\\
1 & 1 & 4 
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

**Which of the following calculations are possible?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can add or subtract two matrices if they have the same dimensions. Otherwise, the operation is not possible.

Matrics $\bold A$ and $\bold D$ are both $2\times 3$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Matrics $\bold B$ and $\bold C$ are both $3\times 2$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Other combinations are not possible.

These are the possible calculations:

- $\bold A-\bold D$
- $\bold B+\bold C$

assessment_items.question_content:
Here are four matrices:

$\begin{align}
\bold A=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
3
\\\\
4
\\\\
5
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold B=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-1 & -3
\\\\
0 & 4
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
\bold C=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2
\\\\
1
\\\\
0
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold D=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-5 & 2
\\\\
3 & -4
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

**Which of the following calculations are possible?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can add or subtract two matrices if they have the same dimensions. Otherwise, the operation is not possible.

Matrics $\bold A$ and $\bold C$ are both $3\times 1$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Matrics $\bold B$ and $\bold D$ are both $2\times 2$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Other combinations are not possible.

These are the possible calculations:

- $\bold A+\bold C$
- $\bold C-\bold A$
- $\bold D-\bold B$

assessment_items.question_content:
Here are four matrices:

$\begin{align}
\bold A=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-5
\\\\
1
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold B=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
3
\\\\
-2
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
\bold C=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
0 & 0
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold D=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
4 & -3
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

**Which of the following calculations are possible?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can add or subtract two matrices if they have the same dimensions. Otherwise, the operation is not possible.

Matrics $\bold A$ and $\bold B$ are both $2\times 1$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Matrics $\bold C$ and $\bold D$ are both $1\times 2$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

All four calculations are possible:

- $\bold A+\bold B$
- $\bold B-\bold A$
- $\bold C-\bold D$
- $\bold D+\bold C$
